title: Social Network for Seniors
body: [![post55-home](http://farm5.static.flickr.com/4058/4681073055_014ef8ddf2.jpg)](http://www.flickr.com/photos/diluvi/4681073055/ "post55-home by diluvi, on Flickr")**One of  [my latest projects](http://www.xarop.com/category/works/) for [Socialtec](http://www.socialtec.es) was designing the brand identity, the screens (user interfaces),  and the various graphic elements that make up the social network geared toward  seniors [post55](http://www.post55.es/ "post55 - community for seniors").** In just a short time since its launch, this new community has generated significant [media coverage](http://www.google.es/search?q=post55 "post55 results on Google") and has attracted a large number of users and strong engagement both on the website itself and on the social media platforms used ([Facebook](http://www.facebook.com/pages/post55/256120848844?ref=ts "post55 on Facebook"), [YouTube](http://www.youtube.com/user/comunidadpost55 "post55 videos on YouTube"), [Twitter](http://twitter.com/comunidadpost55 "post55 on Twitter"), and [Flickr](http://www.flickr.com/groups/post55/ "post55 photos on Flickr")) Despite being a fairly complex platform with many possibilities, efforts have been made to make it **user-friendly** and understandable for new users who are not accustomed to the Internet and [Social Media](http://www.xarop.com/category/social-media/). Video tutorials, interaction, and the simple ability to share experiences with other users all aim to ensure a satisfying experience for members. It is a youthful website and design that seeks to evolve according to the needs of its users; new sections and necessary adjustments to the platform’s design emerge day by day. We can say that it is a vibrant community for both users and developers. [Visit post55.es](http://post55.es "Community for Seniors")
